name: modular-api-with-guards
description: Validate that the skill produces a modular NestJS API with proper module structure, guards for authentication, DTOs with validation, and correct dependency injection patterns.
prompt: Create a NestJS 11 REST API for a task management system. The API has a TasksModule with a TasksController and TasksService. Tasks belong to a Project and are assigned to a User. Implement a JWT AuthGuard that protects all task endpoints, a RolesGuard for admin-only delete, DTOs with class-validator decorators for create and update, and register everything in the TasksModule with proper imports and exports.

assertions.description: Must use @Module() decorator to define the feature module with explicit imports, providers, controllers, and exports arrays.
Must implement the CanActivate interface for the JWT authentication guard to integrate with the NestJS guard lifecycle.
Must apply guards with @UseGuards() decorator on controllers or individual route handlers for authentication and authorization.
Must use ValidationPipe to activate class-validator decorators on incoming DTOs for runtime type safety.
Must mark services and guards with @Injectable() to register them in the NestJS dependency injection container.

name: microservice-communication
description: Validate that the skill produces correct NestJS microservice patterns with transport configuration, message patterns, hybrid application setup, and proper serialization.
prompt: Build a NestJS microservices setup with two services: an API Gateway (HTTP) and an OrderService (TCP transport). The gateway receives HTTP requests and forwards them to OrderService using ClientProxy. OrderService handles a 'create_order' message pattern and emits an 'order_created' event. Show the hybrid application bootstrap, ClientsModule registration, message and event pattern handlers, and DTOs shared between services.

assertions.description: Must use ClientsModule.register() or registerAsync() to configure the microservice client with transport and connection options.
Must use @MessagePattern() decorator for request-response communication between microservices.
Must use @EventPattern() decorator for event-driven fire-and-forget communication between services.
Must specify the transport layer enum (Transport.TCP, Transport.REDIS, etc.) for microservice connection configuration.
Must call connectMicroservice() on the application instance to create a hybrid HTTP + microservice application.
